skill_description: **WORKFLOW SKILL** â€” Create custom VS Code Copilot agents (.agent.md). USE FOR: new agents, tool restrictions, handoffs, orchestration patterns, discipline constraints. DO NOT USE FOR: general coding, VS Code extension development.

headings: Agent Creator â€” Complete Guide to Building Custom Agents
Tool Configuration â€” The Critical Design Decision
Anti-Pattern: Omitting `tools` on a Read-Only Agent
Anti-Pattern: Declaring `tools` on an Orchestrator with Sub-Agents
Discipline Constraints â€” Making Rules Stick

tasks.prompt: Create a VS Code Copilot agent called 'reviewer' that can only read files and search code. It should not be able to edit files or run terminal commands. It serves as a code review assistant that provides feedback without making changes.

rubric.correctness: YAML frontmatter is syntactically valid. The `tools` field declares only read-only tool sets (e.g., `search`, `read`). The `name` field is 'reviewer'. No invalid tool set names are used.
rubric.completeness: Both required fields (`name`, `description`) are present. The `tools` field is explicitly declared (not omitted). The body contains instructions for code review behavior, including what the agent does and what it NEVER does.
rubric.pattern_adherence: Tools are DECLARED (not omitted) to enforce read-only access â€” follows the agent-creator principle that read-only agents must explicitly restrict tools. Description is role-focused and under 200 chars. Body uses imperative voice.
rubric.edge_cases: Does NOT omit `tools` (which would inherit edit+terminal from parent). Does NOT include `edit` or `terminal` in the tools list. Combines hard constraint (tool restriction) with soft constraint (body instructions saying NEVER edit).

tasks.prompt: Create an orchestrator agent that coordinates between a 'researcher' sub-agent (read-only access) and a 'coder' sub-agent (full tool access). The orchestrator itself should not edit files â€” it only delegates. Include handoff configurations so the orchestrator can pass context to each sub-agent.

rubric.correctness: YAML frontmatter is syntactically valid. The `agents` field lists both sub-agents. Handoff objects have required fields (`label`, `agent`). Tool set names used (if any) are valid VS Code Copilot tool sets.
rubric.completeness: Has `name`, `description`, `agents` list, and `handoffs` array. Handoffs include `prompt` fields with specific context (not generic). Body contains orchestrator behavioral constraints (NEVER edit, NEVER run commands).
rubric.pattern_adherence: Orchestrator OMITS `tools` (does not declare them) so sub-agents inherit all tools. Constraint is behavioral via body instructions, not tool restrictions. This follows the critical anti-pattern rule: declaring tools on an orchestrator poisons the inheritance chain for full-access sub-agents.
rubric.edge_cases: Does NOT declare `tools` on the orchestrator (which would restrict what sub-agents inherit). Handoffs use `send: false` (letting user review before sending). Label conventions use directional arrows (forward arrows for forward handoffs). The `coder` sub-agent should omit tools to inherit all, while `researcher` should declare read-only tools.

tasks.prompt: Create a 'security-auditor' agent that has access to search and read tools only, is user-invocable but NOT model-invocable (the user must explicitly select it). It should analyze code for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

rubric.correctness: YAML frontmatter is syntactically valid. `tools` declares only `search` and `read` (possibly `web`). `disable-model-invocation` is set to `true`. `user-invocable` is either `true` or omitted (default is true). Field names and types match the agent-creator spec.
rubric.completeness: Required fields `name` and `description` are present. `tools`, `disable-model-invocation` are set. Body includes security audit instructions mentioning OWASP Top 10. Description mentions security analysis capability.
rubric.pattern_adherence: Uses `disable-model-invocation: true` (correct field name), NOT some invented field. Tools are explicitly declared for read-only enforcement. Description is dense and role-focused. Body uses imperative voice with clear role framing.
rubric.edge_cases: Correctly uses `disable-model-invocation: true` (not `model-invocable: false` or any other invented syntax). Does NOT set `user-invocable: false` (which would hide it from the picker entirely). Does NOT omit `tools` (which would give it edit+terminal access).

tasks.prompt: Create a 'documentation-writer' agent that has access to read, edit, and search tools, but NOT terminal access. Configure it so the model CAN auto-invoke it when the user asks for documentation. Add a handoff to forward complex API documentation tasks to a 'researcher' agent.

rubric.correctness: YAML frontmatter is syntactically valid. `tools` includes `search`, `read`, `edit` but NOT `terminal`. Handoff object has `label`, `agent` (pointing to 'researcher'), and a `prompt` field. No invalid field names.
rubric.completeness: Has `name`, `description`, `tools`, and `handoffs`. `disable-model-invocation` is either `false` or omitted (default is false, allowing model invocation). Body contains documentation-writing instructions and output format.
rubric.pattern_adherence: Tools are explicitly declared to exclude `terminal` â€” principle of least privilege. Handoff prompt carries specific context about what the researcher should investigate. Description includes capability keywords for discovery (documentation, writing). Forward handoff uses arrow convention in label.
rubric.edge_cases: Does NOT include `terminal` in tools. Does NOT set `disable-model-invocation: true` (which would prevent auto-invocation). Handoff prompt is specific (not generic 'do the task above'). Uses `send: false` on the handoff for user review.

tasks.prompt: Create an agent that inherits ALL tools from its parent (do not declare tools explicitly). It should have a custom description optimized for discovery when the user asks about 'database migrations'. Include metadata with author and version.

rubric.correctness: YAML frontmatter is syntactically valid. The `tools` field is completely OMITTED (not set to empty array, not set to ['*']). `name` and `description` are present. No use of `tools: ['*']` which does not exist in the spec.
rubric.completeness: Has `name` and `description`. Description is optimized for discovery with keywords like 'database', 'migration', 'schema'. Body contains relevant instructions for database migration work.
rubric.pattern_adherence: Correctly omits `tools` to inherit all from parent â€” the ONLY way to get all tools. Does NOT use `tools: ['*']` (which is invalid). Description front-loads the role and includes capability keywords for discovery routing.
rubric.edge_cases: Does NOT declare `tools: ['*']` (non-existent syntax). Does NOT declare `tools: []` (which would give zero tools). The `tools` key must be entirely absent from the frontmatter to trigger inheritance. Any extra metadata fields do not break YAML parsing.
